message:
fix(core): comprehensive error propagation, transactional imports, and safety hardening

A broad set of correctness and robustness improvements spanning ID resolution,
import/export, storage, doctor repair, and CLI commands.

Error Propagation:
- Add resolve_fallible() and resolve_all_fallible() to IdResolver, replacing
  unwrap_or(false) / unwrap_or_default() patterns with proper Result propagation
  across all CLI commands (comments, dep, graph, label, lint, show, update)
- SchemaInfo construction in info.rs now returns Result instead of swallowing
  count_issues() and get_all_config() errors

Transactional Import with Rollback:
- Wrap entire import_from_jsonl in BEGIN IMMEDIATE...COMMIT transaction so
  partial imports never corrupt the database; on failure, rollback restores
  the previous state including PRAGMA foreign_keys
- Use new in-transaction variants clear_all_export_hashes_in_tx and
  set_export_hashes_in_tx for atomicity

Auto-flush Path Resolution:
- auto_flush() now accepts explicit jsonl_path parameter instead of hardcoding
  beads_dir.join("issues.jsonl"), respecting CLI overrides via
  discover_beads_dir_with_cli()

Doctor Repair Infrastructure:
- Extract repair logic into shared repair_database_from_jsonl() and generic
  rebuild_database_family_with_backup() in config/mod.rs, with tests for
  original-DB restoration on import failure and issue_prefix inference

Storage Improvements:
- Simplify schema open to always check runtime_schema_compatible(), repairing
  missing indexes even when user_version matches CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION
- Store empty strings instead of NULLs for 14 optional text fields in upsert,
  improving round-trip consistency
- Recompute content_hash for tombstone state in delete_issue; simplify
  purge_issue to remove stale event recording
- Shared parse_blocked_by_json() helper for blocked cache with proper error
  propagation; add rebuild_blocked_cache_in_tx() for transactional rebuild
- Change set_metadata_in_tx and clear_all_export_hashes_in_tx to pub(crate)
  &self methods

History & Security:
- Backup filenames now use microsecond precision with collision suffix counter
- New parse_backup_filename() handles both old and new timestamp formats
- Path traversal hardening via validated_backup_filename() rejecting non-single
  components; atomic write pattern for restore_backup

CLI Deduplication:
- Label deduplication in br q via push_unique_label() helper
- Pre-fetch all IDs into HashSet for ID generation (avoids per-candidate DB queries)
- Label dedup in create/import with post-trim uniqueness check

Redirect Fix:
- Resolve relative redirects relative to .beads dir (not parent), validate final
  path has .beads filename component

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -289,10 +289,10 @@ fn resolve_issue_id(
     input: &str,
 ) -> Result<String> {
     resolver
-        .resolve(
+        .resolve_fallible(
             input,
-            |id| storage.id_exists(id).unwrap_or(false),
-            |hash| find_matching_ids(all_ids, hash),
+            |id| storage.id_exists(id),
+            |hash| Ok(find_matching_ids(all_ids, hash)),
         )
         .map(|resolved| resolved.id)
 }

@@ -214,10 +214,10 @@ fn resolve_target_ids(
         ids.push(last_touched);
     }
 
-    let resolved_ids = resolver.resolve_all(
+    let resolved_ids = resolver.resolve_all_fallible(
         &ids,
-        |id| storage.id_exists(id).unwrap_or(false),
-        |hash| storage.find_ids_by_hash(hash).unwrap_or_default(),

     Ok(resolved_ids.into_iter().map(|r| r.id).collect())
@@ -309,10 +309,10 @@ fn optional_date_field(value: Option<&str>) -> Result<Option<Option<DateTime<Utc
 
 fn resolve_issue_id(resolver: &IdResolver, storage: &SqliteStorage, input: &str) -> Result<String> {
     resolver
-        .resolve(
+        .resolve_fallible(
             input,
-            |id| storage.id_exists(id).unwrap_or(false),
-            |hash| storage.find_ids_by_hash(hash).unwrap_or_default(),
+            |id| storage.id_exists(id),
+            |hash| storage.find_ids_by_hash(hash),
         )
         .map(|resolved| resolved.id)
 }
